text:
There once was an adventurous old owl named Olly. One serene autumn morning, Olly woke to the sound of a gentle breeze rustling through the trees. His eyes twinkled; today was a fine day for exploration.

Olly flew out onto a big branch. Along the way, he spotted a flock of sparrows chatting animatedly. They welcomed him each time Olly passed.

"I've flown everywhere," Olly talked to them. "There's nowhere I wouldn't go!"

As he continued, he heard some rustling in the shadows. He approached cautiously.

There stood a large fox. The fox smirked and gestured invitingly.

"Oh no," Olly thought. "Not that."

But curiosity got the best of him.

"I've been everywhere yourself," Olly said boldly.

The fox laughed louder.

"Just teasing. There's nothing to see here."

Olly resumed his flight, remembering it was best not to engage. One day he'd teach him that.
